pattern: Sort to find the median, then re-sort by strength (distance from median, breaking ties by larger value), and return the first k elements.
hints: The median m is defined as arr[((n-1)/2)] in the sorted array using integer division.
Strength of element x is |x - m|; ties are broken by the larger value winning.
Sort the array by strength descending, then take the first k — the answer can be returned in any order.

edge_inputs.description: k equals length — return all elements
All same elements — any k elements valid
Negative values — abs distance still applies
Even-length array where median index is (n-1)/2 = floor

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    arr = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(size)]
    k = rng.randint(1, size)
    return [arr, k]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def getStrongest(self, arr, k):
        arr_sorted = sorted(arr)
        n = len(arr_sorted)
        m = arr_sorted[(n - 1) // 2]
        arr_sorted.sort(key=lambda x: (-abs(x - m), -x))
        return arr_sorted[:k]
